title: Abstract
content: Rutile TiO 2 nanoparticle modified anatase TiO 2 nanorods (TiO 2 -RMA) with 010 facets exposing structure were one-pot synthesized and characterized by X-ray diffraction (XRD), field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM), and fast-Fourier transform pattern (FFT).It showed high photocatalytic reduction CO 2 conversion than pure anatase TiO 2 nanorods.The total yield of CH 4 of TiO 2 -RMA obtained in the experiment after 8 h of continuous irradiation was 18.9 molg.In comparison, TiO 2 -A shows low photocatalytic activity toward methane production 10.3 molg after 8 h UV light irradiation.And the photocatalytic mechanism of TiO 2 -RMA also was discussed.

title: Experimental
content: 2.1.Preparation of TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA 2.1.1.Sodium titanate (ST) precursor 4 g P25 was put into 80 mL of 10 M NaOH aqueous solution, then transferred into a 100 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave, and sealed.The autoclave was put into an oven, heated at 140 C for 24 h, and cooled naturally in air, centrifuged and subsequently washed with deionized water for several times until the pH reached to 10.5. 1 g ST was dispersed into 38 mL deionized water with 2 mL 30 H 2 O 2 solution, then transferred to a 50 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave, and sealed.The autoclave was put into an oven, heated at 160 C for 24 h, and cooled naturally in air, producing white TiO 2 -A precipitates.TiO 2 -RMA was synthesized without H 2 O 2 solution adding.These white precipitates were isolated from the solution by centrifugation and subsequently washed with deionized water, and finally dried at 60 C in vacuum for 10 h. X-ray diffraction patterns of the samples were recorded at room temperature, by a Bruker D8 advance X-ray diffractometer using Cu K radiation and 2 scan rate of 6 min -1 .Diffraction patterns were taken over the 2 range 20-70.FESEM images were obtained by a JEOL JEM-6700F Field Emission Scanning Electron Microscope. Transmission electron microscopy and high-resolution transmission electron microscopy images were obtained by a JEOL JEM-2010FEF Field Emission Electron Microscope. In the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 , 0.1 g of the TiO 2 catalyst was uniformly dispersed onto the glass reactor with an area of 5.4 cm 2 .A 300 W mercury lamp was used as the light source of the photocatalytic reaction.The volume of the reaction system was about 270 mL.The reaction setup was vacuum-treated several times, and then the high purity of CO 2 gas was followed into the reaction setup for reaching ambient pressure.A total of 1 mL of deionized water was injected into the reaction system as the reducer.To ensure direct contact of the TiO 2 catalyst with CO 2 and gaseous water, the as-prepared photocatalysts were first treated in 500 C to desorb the gas and burn off the surfactant residual on the surface and then allowed to equilibrate in the CO 2 H 2 O atmosphere for several hours to ensure that the adsorption of gas molecules was complete.During irradiation at room temperature, about 0.5 mL of gas was continually taken from the reaction cell at given time intervals for subsequent CH 4 concentration analysis by using a gas chromatograph (GC-14B and GC8A; Shimadzu Corp., Japan).

title: Results and discussion
content: Fig. 1 shows the XRD pattern of TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA.For TiO 2 -A, all XRD peaks of the as-synthesized product can be indexed to anatase TiO 2 (JCPDS No. 00-021-1272) and no rutile phase (JCPDS No. 00-021-1276) was detected.This indicates that TiO 2 -A are all pure anatase TiO 2 .In addition to anatase phase, some rutile phase peaks, such as ( 110), ( 101), ( 111) and ( 211), appear in TiO 2 -RMA sample.It indicates that TiO 2 -RMA has a heterogeneous structure of rutile and anatase TiO 2 . Fig. 2a-d shows the FESEM images of the TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA.It can be seen that there are some TiO 2 nanoparticles loading on TiO 2 nanorods for TiO 2 -RMA sample (Fig. 2a andb).And the size of TiO 2 nanoparticles and nanorods are about 80 nm and 300 nm 2 m, respectively.And for TiO 2 -A sample, the nanorods are with sizes of 300 nm 2 m (Fig. 2c andd).Obviously, the small nanoparticles may be rutile TiO 2 and the nanorods may be anatase TiO 2 . For confirming this result, TEM, HRTEM and FFT characterizations are used as shown in Fig. 3. Firstly, TEM images of TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA (Fig. 3a andb) showed the same morphology and size (nanoparticles modified nanorods for TiO 2 -RMA, and nanorods for TiO 2 -A), which also have been shown by FESEM images.Secondly, for TiO 2 -A, the clearly lattice fringes with lattice spacing of 0.48 and 0.35 nm are corresponding to those of the 002 and 101 facets, respectively (Fig. 3d).And the angle between 101 and 10-1 facets is about 44, and about 68between 101 and 004 facets, which are in agreement with the results from the corresponding FFT pattern (Fig. 3f).Based on the above analysis, it can be concluded that the four rectangle facets of TiO 2 nanorods are 010 facets (010 and 100 facets have the same surface construction, so they are all indexed to 010 facets).Thirdly, Fig. 3c display the HRTEM image of TiO 2 -RMA, which show clearly two lattice fringes with a lattice spacing of 0.32 nm and 0.35 nm, corresponding to that of the rutile 110 and anatase 101 facets.The FFT pattern of Fig. 3c also proved that the clearly lattice fringe of 0.32 nm is the rutile 110 facets.They indicate that the nanoparticles are rutile TiO 2 and the nanorods are anatase TiO 2 . We perform CO 2 and gaseous H 2 O photocatalysis in a gas-solid system over the TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA in the absence of any co-catalysts under UV light ( 365 nm) irradiation.Gas chromatographic analysis shows that CH 4 was exclusively obtained as the reduction product without detectable CH 3 OH, H 2 , and CO as secondary products.The gross yield in CH 4 increased with the photolysis time (Fig. 4), and the total yield of CH 4 of TiO 2 -RMA obtained in the experiment after 8 h of continuous irradiation was 18.9 molg.A conversion experiment of CO 2 performed in the dark or in the absence of the photocatalyst shows no appearance of CH 4 , proving that the reduction reaction of CO 2 is driven by light under the photocatalyst.In comparison, TiO 2 -A shows low photocatalytic activity toward methane production 10.3 molg after 8 h UV light irradiation.And the CH 4 generation of TiO 2 -RMA is very close to that of TiO 2 -A with 1 wt.Pt loading as shown in Fig. 4a. The higher photocatalytic activity of the TiO 2 -RMA toward the reduction of CO 2 than that of the TiO 2 -A can be contributed to the rutile TiO 2 nanoparticle modification.As shown in Fig. 4b, the conduction band (CB) of anatase is more negative than that of the rutile Fig. 3. TEM, HRTEM and FFT images of TiO 2 -RMA (a, c and e) and TiO 2 -A (b, d and f).18,19.Under UV light irradiation, the photo-induced electron of anatase transfer to VB of rutile.And then, the photo-induced electrons and holes can be separated well.So, TiO 2 -RMA showed higher photocatalytic reduction CO 2 conversion than pure anatase TiO 2 nanorods.

title: Conclusions
content: In this letter, rutile TiO 2 nanoparticles were used as an electron acceptor, which can replace the expensive Pt.Rutile TiO 2 nanoparticle modified anatase TiO 2 nanorods with 010 facets exposing structure were one-pot synthesized.And TiO 2 -RMA showed high photocatalytic reduction CO 2 conversion than pure anatase TiO 2 nanorods.These findings also provide a new direction to design efficient photocatalysts for CO 2 photocatalytic reduction in hydrocarbon fuels. j o u r n a l h o m e p a g e : w w w .e l s e v i e r .c o m l o c a t e c a t c o m 2.1.2.TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA Fig. 1.XRD pattern of TiO 2 -A and TiO 2 -RMA. Fig. 2. FESEM images of TiO 2 -RMA (a and b) and TiO 2 -A (c and d). P.-Q.Wang et al. Catalysis Communications 29 (2012) 185-188 This work was supported by The Seventh Installment of Graduate Innovation Fund of Southwest Petroleum University, grant no: GIFSB0704.
